news.title: Library News
news.subtitle: Stay up to date with announcements, new arrivals, and events from our university library.

item1.title: Spring 2026 Book Collection Has Arrived
item1.desc: Over 300 new titles across science, humanities, and technology have been added to our shelves. Visit the New Arrivals section on the ground floor to explore the latest additions.

item2.title: Extended Hours During Finals Week
item2.desc: The library will be open 24 hours a day from April 20 through May 5 to support students during the final examination period. Quiet study zones will be fully staffed.

item3.title: 50,000+ E-Books Now Accessible via New Portal
item3.desc: Our partnership with leading academic publishers gives every enrolled student unlimited access to over 50,000 e-books and 200 research journals through the new Digital Library Portal.

item4.title: Reading Club Kicks Off Its Spring Season
item4.desc: Join fellow students every Thursday at 6 PM in Reading Room B for our popular Reading Club. This semester we explore contemporary Central Asian Literature. All levels welcome.

item5.title: Silent Study Zone Expansion Completed
item5.desc: Following months of renovation, our silent study zone on the third floor now features 80 new ergonomic workstations, improved lighting, and high-speed charging points at every seat.

1984.desc: A dystopian novel about totalitarianism, surveillance, and the cost of truth.

brave_new_world.title: Brave New World
brave_new_world.desc: A futuristic society built on happiness at the cost of freedom and individuality.

crime_and_punishment.title: Crime and Punishment
crime_and_punishment.desc: A psychological drama about guilt, morality, and redemption in 19th-century Russia.

pride_and_prejudice.title: Pride and Prejudice
pride_and_prejudice.desc: A romantic novel of manners following Elizabeth Bennet as she navigates society and love.

the_catcher_in_the_rye.title: The Catcher in the Rye
the_catcher_in_the_rye.desc: A coming-of-age story narrated by the cynical and disenchanted Holden Caulfield.

the_great_gatsby.title: The Great Gatsby
the_great_gatsby.desc: A story of wealth, obsession and the American Dream set in the Jazz Age.

the_hobbit.title: The Hobbit
the_hobbit.desc: Bilbo Baggins embarks on an unexpected quest with a group of dwarves and the wizard Gandalf.

to_kill_a_mockingbird.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
to_kill_a_mockingbird.desc: A classic of modern American literature tackling racial injustice in the Deep South.
